reviews.review_description: People should not look at this for the production value. And this is not a nonfiction biographical documentary. Yeah, it's obviously staged. I get that. It's more of a play. Nonetheless, it is a very emotional and thought-provoking piece, as intended. And what I got out of it was thinking that there are many people out there who have it much worse. There are drugs, sex crimes, and many acts of violence in the world of the homeless. And every one of those people has a story to tell, if you will listen.
very real
Mmmk,so i dont really like documentary things but i saw a horse in the photos so i downloaded and i regret it I CANT UNINSTALL IT HELP!
This was so well done. While its a sad topic, you really feel like you are in a homeless persons shoes! You will feel a connection to Rockey by the end. Sad but eye opening.

reviews.review_title: Hope to see more VR like this
reviews.review_description: I think it is a very good thing making this VR experience. I expected it to be a little longer. Alot of people in today's society ONLY go off of assumptions of how they think things are or about others lives and not off of FACTS. They won't take the time to find out the facts for themselves. It is true ANYONE could be in the situation not all homeless are drug addicts. People who go on about the police clearly have never had anything major or serious happen to them before. I hope you make more VR experiences like this maybe show how truly scary it is for these people especially at night. This is what the VR world needs more of GOOD JOB! I hope she is doing alot better today. Bad things happen to good people and it's sad. Then, they have to be judged by people who know NOTHING about what the person they are judging have even been through and assuming things and not knowing the full story or learning facts.

Hopefully homeless people eventually hear this message through someone.

You don't have to consider yourself homeless. If you consider yourself a professional backpacker, the whole world opens up to you.

Go train hoping on cargo trains to see new beautiful sights and places. For food, get either leftovers from restaurants or reclaimed food. (Yes that's a thing, and no its not just for the homeless. People who live on the extreme on not wasting food do it, too.) You could also hunt and gather.

For camp, stay on federal lands, abandoned places, or even take a nap in the park if you can't find a place and need to stay up all night.

I know I chose this lifestyle, but most people have it forced upon them. I started as a vandweller, now live the RV life, and will soon live the buslife. After that, I'll build a tiny home, and then become a professional backpacker.

I make 6 figures, and I know you don't need much to live on. Those who live on next to nothing get to see the world, and those who weigh themselves down with stuff live in a cage of gold. The secret to happiness is sharing experiences with others, not collecting the next cool tech gadget.
cry
Wonderful yet heartbreaking experience. I wish there was more content like this available on the Oculus.

reviews.review_title: Bycles and homes for the homeless
reviews.review_description: let's raise funds for the homeless, do you care?
As an experience, it's amazing. As some detractors have noted, it is a bit of propaganda and is staged, but as a showcase for what VR can do, that's worth overlooking.

Well done experience that gives you the opportunity understand homelesness better.  It humanizes the issue, teaches empathy. Leaves you with a feeling you learned something.

Good news is the main protagonist Rockey is longer homeless ❤ (I Googled "Rockey Invisible People"). Invisible People is also the name of the charity that helped produce this piece so those wanting to help should visit their website & follow/subscribe their channel. 

Also this isn't story about police it's about homelessness. And Rockey is a real person. Not sure what would motivate someone to give this beautiful story 1 star but let's be kind to each other ❤
Beautiful, moving, honest, powerful. I want to know where to make a donation to help Rocky and others experiencing homelessness. Thank you for this powerful experience. I hope to see more like it.
Brilliantly set up and a very touching story, this was definitely worth a download and a watch. Sad how people’s lives unfold with no fault of their own.

Don't waste your time.  Everything in this is staged and acted.   In the first few seconds you can see that all of her belongings are very clean and put there on purpose.  Give us a real homeless (addiction) situation and it would be worth the experience.

Sorry, these are actors in scripted scenarios, just another documentary to make cops look like the bad guys. Wow, amazing what the devs think we need in these tense times :-/     I was just involved in a toy drive where the cops (gasp!) were collecting for poor children, they do tons to help disadvantaged families, but they get no cred, at least from these devs. The E (Everyone) rating here is also very disingenuous: there are heavy swear words, suggestive sexual material, etc.  Finally, I fully realize homelessness is a heart-wrenching, horrible problem, and often not the fault of the victim at all. We get it. I had a dear friend who was homeless at one period of his life, and I take it extremely seriously. That’s why this scripted documentary, so one-sided at the heart of it, is — in the end — so intellectually dishonest too. A swing and a miss.
This is beautifully crafted masterpiece documentary. It is heart wrenching and bought tears to my eyes. Please make more wonderful thought provoking material like this
This is very heartfelt. It has 3d scenes and vr video and is another example of VR for Good being just that.

reviews.review_title: Take a walk in someone else's shoes
reviews.review_description: Excellent delivery and powerful message. More people need to see what struggling people go through. I cried, man.
It tells an important story in an immersive way.

Meta has done a fantastic job, giving a platform for experiences like this to shine and to be seen by many.
Hopefully they include these kind of “games” in the next Meta Quest Gaming Showcase.

Five stars for the topic, the presentation, the immersion and heart.
